title: practical english usage - Part 105
content:
the outpatients department (of ahospital) the sales department     the arrival(s) hall (at an airport) the accounts department   the drug(s) problem a savings account       an antique(s) dealer/shop arms control          a greeting(s) card a customs officer        a sports car a glasses case          a goods train (British English) a clothes shop          a drinks cabinet structure. Examples: plural forms are found. In general, plurals are becoming more common in this In some cases, e.g. antique(s), drug(s), usage is divided, and both singular and which  are more often used in the plural than in the singular (like savings).

used in the singular with the same meaning (like customs), and some nouns include nouns which  have no singular form (like clothes), nouns which  are not Some nouns are plural in this structure, especially in British English. These 4 exceptions:       first    noun    plural     - a  clothes        shop Exception: three quarters (a three-quarter length coat) not a two-thirds share (     a two-third share) In fractions, the plural -s is not usually dropped.

The  number one is often left out.

a five-day course    a two-person tent   ten two-hour lessons a hundred-dollar bill  a six-pound chicken  a three-mile walk not a ten-pound note (     a ten pounds note) not ɑ  five-litre can (     a five-litres can) first noun is normally singular in form in these cases.

The  number is usually joined to the first noun by a hyphen (-). Note that the Noun + noun is used in measurements, with a number before the first noun.

3 measurement:         a  five-litre        can Section11 Nouns     and   Noun    Phrases;     Agreement grammar •  125  nouns in combination: milk chocolate For more details, ►     126.

cow's milk (     eew-mUk) not letters from home (     home-letters) not a feeling of disappointment (     a-disappeintmentfreling) sometimes a structure with possessive 's is used.

Sometimes it is necessary to use a structure with of or another preposition; Not all compound ideas can be expressed by a noun + noun structure.

written with hyphens (e.g. spectacle-case), but this is now unusual.

write the two words separately. Noun + noun combinations often used to be with very short common combinations like bathroom, it is usually acceptable to station). Sometimes usage varies (e.g. lampshade or lamp shade), but except Other combinations are generally written separately (e.g. furniture shop, railway bathroom toothbrush seaside like single words.

Some short common noun + noun combinations are generally written together 8  spelling For die stressing of road and street names, ►    570.

good dictionary. Note that there are occasional British-American differences.

To  be sure of the stress on a particular combination, it is necessary to check  in a a German 'teacher (adjective modifier: a teacher who is German) a 'German teacher (noun modifier: a person who teaches German) shown by stress. Compare: The  difference between noun modifiers and adjectival modifiers is sometimes a garden chair a fruit 'pie However, there are quite a number of exceptions.

a bicycle factory a 'fruit drink 'ski boots coffee beans Most noun + noun combinations have the main stress on the first noun.

FURNITURE     FACTORY    PAY  CUT   PROTEST it saves space.

This kind of structure is very common in newspaper headlines (►  292) because oil production costs road accident research centre third noun, these can modify a fourth, and so on.

More than two nouns can be put together. A  group of two nouns can modify a 6 more    than    two    nouns grammar •  126   classifying expressions: noun + noun or preposition structure?

The flowers were like a carpet of gold.

He rules his family with a rod of iron.

of silk, a bridge of stone), and it is still used in some metaphorical expressions.
